channel.title: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]
channel.description: Posts in the discussion thread "Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]" - First tale for my character, Dr. Olivia Blackburn.

item.title: Forum Criticism Team - Captain Post
item.content:encoded:
Author, per the required reading forum guidelines: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282684/required-reading:how-to-use-this-forum-ideas please fill out the concept template to seek feedback on your idea.

Remember to please edit your previous post using the "edit" function under the "options" tab to the lower right of your first comment rather than creating a new thread.

Once you have revised your post accordingly, please reach out to reviewers to request that they look at your thread. You can find a list of ways to contact reviewers in this thread: http://www.scp-wiki.net/forum/t-13282692/how-to-get-more-reviews

(Also, that said… you might want to look through some of the character-writing information pages in the Comprehensive Guide Hub if you haven't yet already. The character you have here is already likely to be scrutinized since it seems like a self-insert due to the name being the same as your account name, and the setup of "anomalous researcher hired by Foundation keeps their anomaly a secret" can make the Foundation look incompetent and the character too perfect if not handled carefully.)

item.title: Doctor Blackburn Tale [Seeking Greenlight]
item.content:encoded:
I've had this character in my mind for a while and have been working on a tale for her I would like to publish. Without explaining everything about her, she is a doctor of psychology who works (mostly) with low-threat humanoid SCPs, evaluating their mental states within containment and helping the Foundation better straddle the line between "this is cold but necessary" and "this is unreasonably cruel".
And because this is the Foundation, she has a minor anomalous ability. Extending from various points on people's bodies she can see strings of light no wider than a spider's thread extending, and these threads connect to other people based on familial relation. For example, siblings are all linked by threads extending from their inner left wrists. It's not a story with many practical implications, but in a workplace where knowing something you aren't supposed to can be extremely dangerous she feels very fortunate that she's kept her ability secret since childhood.
The main basis of this little tale is that I want to write about some of her observations and discoveries and the struggles that this knowledge comes with. On one hand a lot of the information she has could be very useful (if SCPs are related to each other, if ones assumed to be 'created' like the Little Misters have signs of parentage or families), but on the other hand knowing intimate family situations of Important People in the Foundation may not be something said people are much of a fan of. And then on a third hand, sometimes she just longs to be gossipy about it all.
It's not an extremely long story, only about 1500 words and mostly completed, but it expresses a lot of important things regarding Olivia's character that I might want to explore more in other ways.

Okay thanks for reading I sure hope I did this right!
